title: malus × moerlandsii
intro: Malus × moerlandsii is a hybrid of crabapple (Malus spp.) and flowering quince (Chaenomeles spp.), known for its fragrant flowers and edible fruits. The medicinal properties of Malus × moerlandsii include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of consuming Malus × moerlandsii include its potential to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, improve cognitive function, and support immune system function. Herbal preparations of Malus × moerlandsii can be made into teas, infusions, and jams, and it is also used as a flavoring agent in traditional medicine.
